steps.title: Identify the key experimental signal
steps.content: The forest seedlings away from fynbos roots grew five times faster—showing that fynbos roots prevent forest plants from thriving in the fynbos habitat.

steps.title: Match this to the border-maintenance mechanism
steps.content: Choice C explains the mechanism correctly: fynbos roots absorb so many nutrients that forest plants cannot flourish nearby, thereby maintaining a sharp border between the habitats. The other three describe root damage, a physical barrier, or benefits to fynbos plants—none of which the experiment supports. That matches choice C — C is correct.
